title: Build frozen audit panels for Glynn-Fox claims audit
close_reason: Completed

title: export_bundle does not constrain manifest asset paths to project_root or bundle
description: manifest.py:216-222 blindly joins AssetRecord.path into both the source and destination paths. If a manifest contains an absolute path, Path joining ignores project_root/output_dir entirely; if it contains ../ segments, the copy target can escape assets/. Because recipe-export accepts an arbitrary manifest file, a malformed or external manifest can copy files from outside the project tree and write outside the bundle directory. Normalize and reject non-relative or escaping asset paths before copying.
close_reason: Fixed: export_bundle now validates asset paths are relative and resolve within project_root and bundle directory. Added regression tests for absolute paths and ../ traversal.

title: One-shot coclab status/doctor readiness command
description:
Add coclab status/doctor command for one-shot environment readiness

Problem
- Session orientation requires manually checking multiple directories/commands.
- Agents need a single readiness report for assets, builds, and missing prerequisites.

Scope
- Add a new command (e.g., `coclab status` or `coclab doctor`) that reports in one run:
  - available curated assets and detected vintages
  - available builds and basic health
  - recipe/build prerequisite checks (present vs missing)
- Include actionable remediation hints for missing inputs.
- Support machine-readable output (`--json`) with stable schema.

Acceptance Criteria
- One command returns a consolidated readiness report.
- Non-zero exit code when required prerequisites are missing (or explicit health status field in JSON).
- Human-readable and JSON outputs are both covered by tests.
- CLI reference docs updated with examples.

Out of Scope
- Auto-fixing missing assets.
- Long-running deep validation (keep command fast).
close_reason: done

title: Enable period-faithful alignment for historical panels
description:
## Summary
Fix infrastructure issues preventing period-faithful temporal alignment (P{Y}@B{Y} with A{Y-1}) for historical panel data.

## Background
When building the GBC-Analysis-ko9 panel, we discovered several issues that forced a fallback to retrospective alignment (using B2025/A2023 for all years). This epic tracks the work to enable true period-faithful alignment.

## Problems Discovered
1. **Tract geography mismatch**: ACS 2010-2019 uses 2010 tract GEOIDs, but crosswalks use T2023 (2020 census)
2. **Missing population weights**: Crosswalks have `pop_share: None`, forcing area-only weighting
3. **Registry corruption**: Boundary downloads sometimes register with temp directory paths
4. **Naming inconsistency**: Measures files have inconsistent naming between writer and reader

## Child Issues
- CoC-PIT-4da: Ingest Census tract relationship file (2010↔2020)
- CoC-PIT-925: Add tract vintage translation layer for ACS ingestion
- CoC-PIT-1sw: Enable population weighting in crosswalk builder CLI
- CoC-PIT-f6w: Fix boundary registry corruption from temp directories
- CoC-PIT-716: Standardize measures file naming convention

## Success Criteria
```bash
# Should work end-to-end without manual intervention
coclab build-panel --years 2015-2024 --alignment period-faithful
```

With period-faithful alignment:
- P2015 uses B2015 boundaries and A2014 (ACS 2010-2014) demographics
- P2020 uses B2020 boundaries and A2019 (ACS 2015-2019) demographics
- etc.

## Impact
- More accurate historical analysis
- Proper temporal alignment for trend studies
- Reduced need for manual workarounds
close_reason: All infrastructure issues resolved: tract relationship ingestion, translation layer, population weighting, registry corruption fix, and measures naming. Period-faithful alignment is now the default policy.

title: CoC ACS fallback still loads incompatible same-boundary measures files
description:
Review follow-up from the 2026-03-27 closed-bugs audit.

The bug bead `coclab-i2fj.8.1` was closed with a note saying the CoC ACS fallback was limited to exact tract-vintage matches, but `_load_acs_measures()` still falls back to the first `measures__A*@B{boundary}` file without checking requested ACS vintage or tract vintage.

Current code path:
- `coclab/panel/assemble.py` lines around 616-625 still do `measures_dir.glob(f"measures__A*@B{boundary_vintage}*.parquet")`
- The first same-boundary file is accepted and loaded
- `tract_vintage_used` is then read from that file's provenance, even if it does not match the requested ACS alignment

Direct repro:
- Create only `measures__A2023@B2024xT2020.parquet`
- Call `_load_acs_measures('2024', '2015-2019', 'area', measures_dir)`
- Observed result: the loader returns that A2023/T2020 file instead of failing, even though `2015-2019` implies 2010-era tracts

This means CoC panel assembly can still bind incompatible ACS inputs silently.
close_reason: Removed fallback that silently loaded measures files with wrong ACS vintage. Now returns empty instead of binding incompatible data.

title: Add --start/--end year filters to coclab ingest pep
description: Add --start/--end to coclab ingest pep (years only, like zori). Parse/validate year ints, enforce start<=end, and reject ranges outside available data (or error on empty). Ensure output file naming/caching doesn’t collide with unfiltered runs (consider suffix with year range or disable cache when filters set). Record requested year filters in provenance and CLI output.
close_reason: Closed

title: Align PEP coverage threshold defaults/docs (0.95 vs 0.90)
description: Spec describes flagging coverage < 0.95, but aggregate/CLI default to 0.90. Decide intended default, update code, docs, and tests to match.
close_reason: Aligned coverage defaults/docs to 0.95 in aggregate/CLI/spec; added test for non-renormalized aggregation.

title: Recipe ZORI panel policy does not canonicalize aggregated zori measure
description:
Review of coclab-gude found that recipe-native ZORI policy only runs when the assembled panel already contains `zori_coc` (`coclab/recipe/executor.py:1893`). Real recipe examples still aggregate county-native ZORI as `zori` (`recipes/examples/coc-base-pit-acs-zori-2016-2021.yaml:164-170`, `recipes/examples/metro-glynnfox-pit-acs-pep-zori-2016-2024.yaml:173-179`). In a targeted repro, the built panel preserved the actual rent in a stray `zori` column while the canonical contract columns `zori_coc`, `zori_is_eligible`, `rent_to_income`, and provenance stayed null.

Required follow-up:
- Canonicalize/rename recipe-native aggregated ZORI data before policy application, or otherwise make `panel_policy.zori` work with the actual county->target recipe path.
- Add regression coverage for the real aggregate+join path, not just identity input fixtures that already contain `zori_coc`.
- Validate the committed example recipes against the canonical panel contract.

Acceptance criteria:
1. A recipe that aggregates county-native `zori` to CoC or metro and declares `panel_policy.zori` produces populated canonical ZORI contract columns (`zori_coc`, eligibility/provenance, `rent_to_income`) with no silent fallback to a stray `zori` column unless an explicit output alias requests it.
2. Regression tests cover the realistic county-native aggregate path used by the committed example recipes.
3. Example recipes that claim ZORI panel semantics build the canonical contract end-to-end.
close_reason: Canonicalize recipe-native zori→zori_coc before policy application; 4 regression tests added

title: Fix default tract vintage for aggregate acs
close_reason: Closed

title: Agent C: Aggregation Engine (county→CoC)
description:
Deliverables:
- coclab/rents/aggregate.py
- Load normalized ZORI, CoC↔county crosswalk, county weights
- Compute monthly CoC ZORI with coverage/dominance metrics per §5.2
- Emit parquet + provenance per schema §4.2
- Functions: aggregate_monthly(zori_df, xwalk_df, weights_df) -> coc_zori_df
- aggregate_zori_to_coc(...) -> Path
close_reason: Implemented aggregate.py with all required functions

title: Optional global base asset catalog support
description: Add optional data/registry/base_assets.json inventory integration so build create can resolve assets from a catalog before pinning into build manifest.
close_reason: Global base asset catalog is premature and misframed. The two real needs are recipe-scoped: (1) asset caching for iteration speed, and (2) provenance/replication bundles. The existing source registry + build manifest already cover cross-recipe provenance. Replaced by coclab-3jsk (recipe-level asset caching) and coclab-1rp1 (recipe-level provenance/replication export).

title: Handle Connecticut planning region GEOID remap for ACS/ZORI post-2022
description:
## Problem
Connecticut ACS 2022+ tract GEOIDs use new county-equivalent (planning region) FIPS codes, while existing crosswalks/tract vintages use legacy county codes. This yields 0% tract matches (state 09) when aggregating measures and breaks ZORI alignment.

## Goal
Add a translation layer that remaps CT tract GEOIDs from legacy county codes to 2022 planning region codes when ACS vintage >= 2022, and provide a concordance to map county-based ZORI to planning regions using tract/population weights.

## Ideas
- Build CT county->planning region crosswalk via spatial join of 2020 tracts to planning region boundaries.
- Recompute tract GEOIDs with new county-equivalent codes before aggregation.
- Use tract population/housing units as weights to translate ZORI county values to planning regions.

## Validation
- CT tract overlap should be near 100% after remap.
- Aggregate measures for CT should have expected coverage_ratio > 0.95.
close_reason: Added CT planning-region remap utilities, wired ACS measures and ZORI aggregation to align CT geography, and added tests.

title: Simplify build base asset directory structure
description:
The current structure for pinned boundary assets is overly nested:

Current: builds/<build>/base/coc_boundary/<year>/coc__B<year>.parquet

The year-specific subdirectory serves no purpose since:
1. The year is already encoded in the filename (coc__B2015.parquet)
2. Consumer code reads paths from the manifest's relative_path field, not by navigating directories
3. There's only one file per year (no supplementary files that would justify a subdirectory)

Proposed simpler structure: builds/<build>/base/coc__B<year>.parquet

The filename uses coc__B prefix (not boundaries__B) since:
- 'coc' identifies the type of boundary (CoC boundaries vs other boundary types)
- The 'B' suffix already indicates it's a boundary vintage
- This matches the preferred naming convention in coclab/naming.py (coc_base_filename)

Changes needed:
- coclab/builds.py: _pin_boundary_assets() - change dest_dir to just 'base' instead of 'base/coc_boundary/<year>'
- Tests that verify the directory structure (test_builds_cli.py, test_build_manifest.py, test_aggregate_cli.py)

Note: This is a breaking change for existing builds. Consider whether migration is needed or if it's acceptable to require recreating builds.
close_reason: Simplified base asset dir: base/coc_boundary/{year}/ → base/ flat structure

title: LAUS CLI returns exit 0 for partial backfill failures in --json mode
description:
Live/synthetic verification on 2026-04-09 shows `coclab ingest laus-metro --start-year ... --end-year ... --json` exits with status 0 even when one or more requested years fail. The command prints `{ "status": "partial", ... }` but still returns success, which makes CI/agent callers treat the backfill as successful unless they parse JSON status manually.

Repro:
- Patch `coclab.ingest.bls_laus.ingest_laus_metro` so one year succeeds and one year raises `ValueError`
- Invoke `typer.testing.CliRunner().invoke(app, ["ingest", "laus-metro", "--start-year", "2021", "--end-year", "2022", "--json"])`
- Observed result: `exit_code == 0` and JSON status `partial`

Likely fix:
- In JSON mode, raise `typer.Exit(1)` whenever `errors` is non-empty, after emitting the structured payload.
- Add CLI coverage for partial/all-failure backfill cases in JSON mode.
close_reason: Fixed: JSON mode now raises typer.Exit(1) when errors is non-empty, after emitting the structured payload; status field now uses 'error' when all years fail. Added CLI tests for partial and all-failed backfill cases.

title: Fix aggregate zori to use boundary year as hub
description:
The aggregate zori command has the same issue as aggregate acs (CoC-PIT-7t7):

Currently uses the latest boundary year (e.g., 2024) for ALL aggregations. Should iterate over build years and use each as the boundary vintage (hub).

Current (wrong):
- Single file: zori__A2023@B2024xC2024__wrenter.parquet

Correct (one per boundary year):
- zori__A2015@B2015xC2015__wrenter.parquet
- zori__A2016@B2016xC2016__wrenter.parquet
- ...
- zori__A2024@B2024xC2024__wrenter.parquet

For each boundary year (hub):
- Boundary vintage = build year
- ACS vintage for weights = derived from build year (matching the alignment logic)
- County vintage = should match boundary year

Fix needed in coclab/cli/aggregate.py aggregate_zori command - loop over build years instead of using single latest boundary.

Related: CoC-PIT-7t7 (same fix for aggregate acs)
close_reason: ZORI loops over build years as boundary hub, derives county/ACS vintages per year

title: Add Connecticut county-transition guardrails and explicit build messaging
description:
Near-term fix for `coclab-2kag` and release guardrail for `coclab-ybc0`.

Goal:
Make county-geometry Connecticut handling visible to both humans and agents, and stop green builds from hiding broken CT rows.

Scope:
- Detect when a recipe mixes CT legacy-county crosswalks with CT planning-region county datasets or support weights.
- Surface that detection in preflight/build output (`--json` and human-readable CLI), including whether CT alignment was applied or whether the build is falling back to a guardrail.
- If required CT alignment cannot be performed, fail loudly or explicitly exclude CT rows by policy; do not emit silent near-zero population values or null weighted measures.

Expected messaging:
- State that Connecticut is being handled as a special case because of the Census county-equivalent transition.
- Include concrete guidance on what artifact or normalization step is missing when the special-case path cannot run.
- Make the applied special-case visible enough that downstream agents do not mistake it for ordinary county processing.

Validation:

- Ensure the example recipe QA path shows the special-case handling in its diagnostics.

title: Recipe metro_name backfill skips present-but-null values
description:
Problem
- _canonicalize_panel_for_target() only fills metro_name when the column is absent.
- If upstream joins or transforms already produce a metro_name column full of nulls, the current backfill is skipped and parquet output still carries null metro_name values.

Repro
- Construct a metro-target panel DataFrame with geo_id values and metro_name=[None, None].
- Call _canonicalize_panel_for_target(..., GeometryRef(type="metro", source="glynn_fox_v1")).
- metro_name remains null instead of being populated from metro_id.

Impact
- Recipe-built metro panels can still persist missing metro names even after the current local patch, depending on upstream shape.

Suggested fix
- Fill metro_name when the column is missing OR contains nulls, using metro_id/geo_id as the fallback key.
- Add a regression test on the recipe execution path that asserts persisted parquet includes populated metro_name values.
close_reason: Fixed: backfill metro_name when column is present but contains nulls; added regression tests

title: interpolate_to_month crashes when the declared year column is string-typed
description:
Review finding from commits on 2026-03-30.

The new generic temporal filter path in `coclab/recipe/executor.py` assumes the declared year column supports integer arithmetic:
- `df_prev[yr_col] = df_prev[yr_col] + 1`
- `df_curr_shifted[yr_col] = df_curr_shifted[yr_col] - 1`

That works for numeric PEP fixtures, but the executor elsewhere already supports string year columns (for example ACS-style vintages and string-backed `year` columns). With a string year column, `interpolate_to_month` raises `TypeError: can only concatenate str (not "int") to str` before the merge step.

Repro:
```python
df = pd.DataFrame({
    "geo_id": ["A", "A"],
    "year": ["2019", "2020"],
    "reference_date": pd.to_datetime(["2019-07-01", "2020-07-01"]),
    "value": [10.0, 20.0],
})
_apply_temporal_filter(..., method="interpolate_to_month", year_column="year")
```

Observed result:
- Raises `TypeError` in the new interpolation branch.

Expected result:
- Either coerce the declared year column to a numeric interpolation key and preserve the original type on output, or raise a clear `ExecutorError` up front.

Code refs:
- coclab/recipe/executor.py:747
- coclab/recipe/executor.py:754
- coclab/recipe/executor.py:758

Suggested fix:
- Normalize the interpolation key to an integer series before shifting years.
- Add regression tests covering string-backed year columns for both target_month < source_month and target_month > source_month.
close_reason: Coerce year column to int before arithmetic and restore original dtype. Also fixed pre-existing bug in target_month > source_month branch where year shifts cancelled out.

title: WP-3F: Panel Assembly Engine
description:
Build analysis-ready CoC x year panels.

Tasks:
1. Load PIT counts
2. Join boundary-aware ACS measures
3. Apply alignment policies
4. Attach diagnostics and flags
5. Persist panel Parquet

Panel Schema columns:
- coc_id, year, pit_total, pit_sheltered, pit_unsheltered
- boundary_vintage_used, acs_vintage_used, weighting_method
- total_population, adult_population, population_below_poverty

description: Produce a concrete extraction plan for the high-risk executor.py persistence path: _assemble_panel, _persist_outputs, _persist_diagnostics, and the ZORI/ACS1/LAUS policy-specific branches. Recommend target modules, data contracts, and compatibility boundaries before moving code so the refactor does not mix policy translation with I/O and orchestration. Cross-check the current callers and the policy-heavy coverage in tests/test_recipe.py and tests/test_recipe_panel_policies.py.

- tract_vintage_used — which tract vintage was used for the crosswalk

The descriptive table immediately below the ER diagram (lines 233–252) correctly includes both columns, creating an internal inconsistency within the same section. The ER diagram should be updated to include these two fields.

title: Aliased recipe panels skip LAUS conformance columns
description:
Review follow-up from coclab-7isb.

When a recipe target uses panel column aliases, executor.py populates PanelRequest.measure_columns with the aliased ACS set before calling run_conformance. conformance._effective_measure_columns() then returns that explicit list early and never adds LAUS columns even when include_laus=True.

Result: a metro recipe panel with panel_policy.laus.include=true can pass conformance while labor_force/employed/unemployed are missing, as long as the aliased ACS columns are present.

Evidence:
- coclab/recipe/executor.py rewrites measure_columns through panel aliases.
- coclab/panel/conformance.py returns request.measure_columns immediately.
- Review reproduction: run_conformance() passes with include_laus=True against a panel that has aliased ACS columns and no labor_force/employed/unemployed columns.

Expected:
- LAUS-aware conformance must still validate LAUS columns when requested, including aliased recipe panels.
- Add a regression test that executes the aliased recipe/conformance path rather than only testing bare _effective_measure_columns() behavior.

Acceptance:
- include_laus=True still validates LAUS measure columns even when panel aliases are present.
- tests cover a recipe or conformance scenario with aliases and LAUS enabled.
close_reason: done

title: Add recipe-plan command for plan-without-execute
description: Add coclab build recipe-plan --recipe <path> that resolves the execution plan and outputs it without executing. Shows resolved tasks (materialize, resample, join), input paths, effective geometries, transform selections, and expected outputs. Supports --json for machine-readable output. Lets agents verify prerequisites and understand the build graph before mutating anything. The planner (resolve_plan) already produces structured ExecutionPlan — this command exposes it.
close_reason: Added recipe-plan command that resolves and displays execution plans with full task details, paths, geometries, and transform selections

title: aggregate zori fails when zori__county only in global data

title: REVIEW: Should panel track tract_vintage_used column?
description:
## Observation
The panel schema in coclab/panel/assemble.py tracks:
- boundary_vintage_used ✓
- acs_vintage_used ✓

But NOT:
- tract_vintage_used ✗

However, ACS measures ARE computed using a tract crosswalk (coc_tract_xwalk__{boundary}__{tract}.parquet), so the tract vintage IS relevant to how the ACS data was aggregated.

## Current Behavior
The tract_vintage is captured in:
- The crosswalk file provenance
- The measures file provenance

But not propagated to the final panel.

## Question
Should the panel include `tract_vintage_used` for full reproducibility, or is it sufficient that the provenance chain captures this?

## Reference
temporal-terminology.md mentions compound notation like `A2022@B2025×T2023` where T2023 is the tract vintage - suggesting it's considered part of the key methodology.
close_reason: Implemented tract_vintage_used column in panel schema. The tract vintage is now extracted from measures file provenance metadata and included in the panel for full reproducibility.

title: Wire glynn_fox_v1 ACS input to year-specific file_set coverage
description:
The glynn_fox_v1 recipe currently points acs_tract at a single static file (data/curated/acs/acs5_tracts__A2024xT2020.parquet) while requesting a 2015-2024 universe. Runtime validation now rejects that implicit broadcast, which prevents bad output but leaves the recipe unable to build.

Follow-up work:
- Define the intended ACS vintage mapping for each analysis year in the recipe.
- Materialize or locate the required yearly ACS tract artifacts.
- Convert acs_tract from a static path to a file_set with correct path template / overrides and geometry vintages.
- Rebuild the panel and verify total_population and median_household_income vary appropriately across years.

Context:
- Available local ACS artifacts currently do not cover the full 2015-2024 range needed by the recipe.
- This was surfaced while fixing coclab-1492 so the pipeline fails loudly instead of broadcasting one ACS snapshot across all years.
close_reason: Materialized yearly ACS tract artifacts for 2015-2024, ingested TIGER 2010 tracts, built data/curated/xwalks/xwalk__B2025xT2010.parquet, and rebuilt recipes/glynn_fox_v2.yaml successfully with year-varying ACS outputs.

title: Connecticut county key drift corrupts county-based CoC recipe outputs from 2021 onward
description:
Reproduction:
1. Run `COCLAB_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 uv run coclab build recipe --recipe recipes/examples/coc-pep-zori-calendar-2020-2024.yaml --json`.
2. Inspect `data/curated/panel/panel__Y2020-2024@B2025.parquet`.
3. Compare with `data/curated/panel/panel__Y2016-2021@B2025.parquet` for the same CoCs.

Observed:
- `CT-503` population is about 706,100 in 2020, then about 0.00058 in 2021-2024.
- `CT-505` population is about 2,343,469 in 2020, then about 0.00077 in 2021-2024.
- `zori` is present for both CoCs in 2020 and null for 2021-2024.
- The broader `coc_base_pit_acs_zori_2016_2021` example shows the same break in 2021.

Root-cause evidence:
- `data/curated/pep/pep_county__v2024.parquet` uses Connecticut IDs `09110`-`09190` (planning regions).
- `data/curated/zori/zori__county__Z2026.parquet` still uses county FIPS `09001`-`09015`.
- The CoC county crosswalk used by the recipe is still the county-2020 crosswalk (`data/curated/xwalks/xwalk__B2025xC2020.parquet`).

Impact:
County-driven CoC example recipes silently produce corrupt Connecticut population values and missing ZORI after the file-set switches to the newer PEP vintage.

Likely fix direction:
Normalize post-2020 Connecticut county-equivalent IDs, or add an explicit compatibility mapping when county-based datasets/crosswalks are combined across vintages.
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement pop_share in tract crosswalk or document it as area-only
description:
## Problem

`coclab/xwalks/tract.py:144` always sets `pop_share` to `None` in every crosswalk produced:

```python
crosswalk = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        geo_id_col: intersections[geo_id_col],
        "boundary_vintage": boundary_vintage,
        "tract_geoid": intersections["GEOID"],
        "tract_vintage": tract_vintage,
        "area_share": intersections["area_share"],
        "pop_share": None,  # Placeholder for v2 with population weighting
        "intersection_area": intersections["intersection_area"],
        "tract_area": intersections["tract_area"],
    }
)
```

This null column ships in every crosswalk parquet artifact. The downstream consumer `coclab/measures/acs.py` references `pop_share` as a weighting option (line 19 in the module docstring) but the `aggregate_to_geo()` function at line ~345 only uses `area_share` because `pop_share` is always null.

## Decision needed

**Option A (Implement population weighting):** Compute `pop_share` using tract population data. The formula is:
```
pop_share = (tract_population * area_share) / sum(tract_population * area_share) per geo
```
This requires tract population data to be available at crosswalk build time. The ACS tract population data (`total_population` column) from `coclab/acs/ingest/tract_population.py` could be joined to the crosswalk. The `build_tract_crosswalk()` function signature would need a new optional `tract_population_df` parameter.

**Option B (Remove the column):** Drop `pop_share` from the crosswalk schema entirely. Update:
1. `coclab/xwalks/tract.py` — remove `pop_share` from the DataFrame constructor (~line 144) and from `build_coc_tract_crosswalk()` which calls the same pattern
2. `coclab/measures/acs.py` — remove references to `pop_share` in docstrings
3. Any tests that assert on crosswalk column schemas: `grep -r "pop_share" tests/`
4. `coclab/xwalks/__init__.py` — check exports

**Option C (Keep column, document as future):** If population weighting is planned soon, just add a clear docstring note. Lowest effort but leaves the null column in artifacts.

## Files involved

- `coclab/xwalks/tract.py:137-148` — the crosswalk builder that sets `pop_share: None`
- `coclab/xwalks/tract.py:156+` — `build_coc_tract_crosswalk()` wrapper
- `coclab/measures/acs.py:19` — docstring referencing pop_share as a weighting option
- `coclab/measures/acs.py:~345` — `aggregate_to_geo()` aggregation logic
- Tests: `grep -r "pop_share" tests/` to find affected test assertions

## Acceptance criteria

- No null-only column ships in crosswalk artifacts, OR pop_share is computed with real values
- Docstrings accurately reflect what weighting options actually work
- `uv run --extra dev pytest` passes
close_reason: Removed always-null pop_share from build_tract_crosswalk; add_population_weights still provides it when needed

title: audit_panels validation still misses missing leading or trailing workload years
description:
Review follow-up from the 2026-03-27 closed-bugs audit.

`coclab/audit_panels.py` added a `year_contiguity` check, but it still computes the expected range from the observed min/max years in the input panel. That catches interior gaps like 2015/2017, but it still accepts panels that are missing the required leading or trailing workload years entirely.

Current code path:
- `_validate_raw_panel()` builds `expected_years = range(all_years[0], all_years[-1] + 1)`
- It never checks the workload's required window (for Workload A, 2015-2024)

Direct repro:
- Build a balanced panel with years 2016-2024 for every geo
- Call `_validate_raw_panel(..., spec=AUDIT_PANEL_SPECS[0], drop_reasons={})`
- Observed result: `structurally_valid == True` and `issues == []`

This leaves the original bug only partially fixed: missing first/last years are still not detected.
close_reason: Added start_year/end_year to AuditPanelSpec; validation now checks against declared audit window, catching missing leading/trailing years.

title: Implement default recipe adapter registration and core geometry/dataset adapters
description:
Recipe dry-run currently fails semantic validation because adapter registries are empty at runtime (no default geometry or dataset adapters are registered). Implement built-in adapter registration and core adapters needed by current recipes.

Scope:
- Provide default registration bootstrap for recipe adapters
- Add geometry adapters for coc/tract/county
- Add dataset adapters for hud/pit, census/acs5 + census/acs, zillow/zori
- Wire registration into recipe CLI path
- Add tests/docs to keep behavior stable
acceptance_criteria:
- Running 'coclab build recipe --recipe recipes/test.yaml --dry-run' no longer emits 'No geometry adapter registered' or 'No dataset adapter registered' for supported types/products
- Unsupported geometry/provider-product combinations still produce clear errors
- Adapter registration is idempotent (safe to call multiple times)
- Tests cover registry bootstrap + adapter validation behavior
close_reason: All child tasks implemented: bootstrap module, geometry adapters (coc/tract/county), dataset adapters (hud-pit, census-acs5, census-acs, zillow-zori), wired into CLI, tests passing

title: Wire conformance checks into build pipeline
description:
Integrate the conformance report into the panel build CLI and recipe executor. Depends on coclab-396b, coclab-2jtk, coclab-1gmj, coclab-2o8i.

Changes:
1. coclab/cli/build_panel.py: After build_panel() returns, construct a PanelRequest from the CLI arguments and run run_conformance(). Print the summary. If any errors, exit with non-zero code. If only warnings, print them and continue.
2. coclab/recipe/executor.py: After recipe execution completes and the panel is written, run conformance checks. Include results in the recipe execution report / manifest.
3. Add --strict flag to CLI: when set, treat warnings as errors (exit non-zero on any conformance issue).
4. Add --skip-conformance flag to CLI: skip all post-build checks (for debugging or known-incomplete builds).
5. Conformance report should be included in save_panel() provenance metadata under extra['conformance'] so it persists with the artifact.
6. When --json is used, conformance report is included in the JSON output.

Tests: integration tests that build a panel with missing years and verify the conformance report is generated and printed.
close_reason: Wired conformance into CLI (--strict, --skip-conformance), recipe executor, and save_panel() provenance with 10 integration tests
